Danger Zone Avoidance
fields.definition: These values govern how we should avoid danger zones (like grenades and vehicles). If their sum is less than 1 then neither action will occur and instead we'll just try to walk out of the way with that remaining probability.

fields.name: armor lock chance#Chance the AI will use their armor lock equipment, assuming they have it
grenade stuck armor lock chance#Chance the AI will use their armor lock equipment if they have been stuck with a grenade, assuming they have it
armor lock safety duration#The number of seconds we will stay in armor lock for after danger has passed (default 1 second)
armor lock max duration#The longest we will stay in armor lock for regardless of danger (default 5 seconds)
armor lock cooldown#We won't go into armor lock again for this many seconds (default 0 seconds)
dive grenade chance
brace grenade chance
obstacle leap min size
obstacle leap max size
obstacle ignore size
obstacle smashable size
RQEMJY
jump height
maximum leap height:wus#.How high can a crate be for this unit to leap it.
leap proximity fraction:[0-1]#How close to the obstacle should the actor be before leaping 1- too close, 0- as soon as he becomes aware of it
avoidance volume turn penalty distance:wus#The maximum distance penalty applied to an avoidance volume search path if we're facing away from the path. 1000 wu good for space, 5 wu good for ground.
movement hints
Throttle and inertia
minimum movement ticks:ticks#We will move at least this long in a single direction when starting movement
minimum movement ticks reset angle:degrees#If the character changes movement direction by more than this angle, he will have to move for at least minimum movement ticks until he can change his mind.
change direction pause
maximum throttle:[0-1]#The character will never throttle beyond this value
minimum throttle:[0-1]#The character will not throttle below this value
movement throttle control
minimum juke throttle:[0-1]#The character will consider juking at this throttle and above
minimum direction change juke angle:deg#If we change movement direction by more this angle, we will attempt a juke

non direction change juke probability#Probability to do a juke for a given tick, even if you are not planning to change direction (and provided you have not already performed a juke within the timeout time

non direction change juke timeout:seconds#After you do a change or no change of direction juke, you cannot perform a NON directional change juke for at least this many seconds. Direction change jukes will still happen
minimum post juke movement ticks:ticks#How many ticks should the actor keep moving after a juke? This may lower juke frequency.
stationary turn radius:[wu]#If this actor translates during turn animations, enter a radius that encloses the translation.
localized move distance:[wu]#Distance to move as per the move_localized firing position evaluator (0 value resolves to 5wu)
move distance:[wu]#Distance to move as per the move_distance firing position evaluator (0 value resolves to 5wu for min, 10wu for max)
vehicle move distance:[wu]#Distance to move as per the vehicle_move_distance firing position evaluator (0 value resolves to 5wu for min, 10wu for max)
turn and run distance from target:wus#Actor will face away from his target and run to his destination if his target at a larger distance than this
turn and run distance to destination:wus#Firing point must be at least this distance away from the actor for him to consider turning and running to it

follow unit buffer distance:wus#When following a unit, such as the player, this is the additional buffer outside of the task follow radius that we are allowed to position ourselves before full firing position avoidance kicks in

fields.definition: These values tell the actor to remain stationary for the specified number of ticks if he changes his direction of movement by at least 'change angle'. Specifying an angle of 0 means pause this much every time you get to your destination.

DESIGNERS BEWARE
fields.definition: If you want to screw the AI up, here's a great place to start. Please try not to.

fields.name: evaluators
EVALUATOR
fields.definition: The firing point evaluation properties for a single evaluator. Treat with great care and respect.

fields.name: evaluator^
Default Values
preference weight
avoidance weight
ready time bounds#Character will pause for given time before engaging threat
flags
Reposition bounds:s#How long should I remain at a firing position before moving? (0 values will use the default values of 6 and 7 seconds)
Crouch danger threshold#When danger rises above the threshold, the actor crouches
Stand danger threshold#When danger drops below this threshold, the actor can stand again.
Fight danger move threshold#When danger goes above given level, this actor switches firing positions
Fight danger move threshold cooldown:ticks#Wait at least this many ticks before relocating due to danger
fight flank chance:[0-1]#Chance of flanking when fighting someone who isn't paying attention to me
override grenade projectile#when I throw a grenade, forget what type I officially have: throw this type of projectile instead
minimum throw distance#Targets closer than this distance will not be attacked with a throw
maximum throw distance#Targets beyond this distance will not be attacked with a throw
throw search radius#How far does actor search for throwable items
throw search angle#Angle (degrees) that the actor searches for throwable items (from his facing direction)
maximum throw force#Maximum throw force - it will not be used all the time
throw target point offset#Vertical offset from target position on ground where throw is aimed
throw delay min#How many seconds MUST pass until another throw is attempted
throw delay max#Up to how many seconds can elapse until another throw is attempted
default combat range:wus#If we are not holding a weapon, or we don't know how to use our weapon, use these bounds on my combat range
default firing range:wus#If we don't know how to use our weapon, use these bounds on my firing range
Charge flags
melee consider range
melee chance#chance of initiating a melee within a 1 second period
melee attack range

melee armor lock delay:seconds#don't attempt a melee on a recently armor locked target for this many seconds
melee leap range
melee leap chance
ideal leap velocity
max leap velocity
melee leap ballistic
melee delay timer:seconds#time between melee leaps
leader abandoned berserk chance#chance for a leader to berserk when all his followers die (actually charge, NOT berserk, but I'm not changing the name of the variable)
shield-down berserk chance#lower bound is chance to berserk at max range, upper bound is chance to berserk at min range, requires shield depleted berserk impulse
shield-down berserk ranges
friendly killed max berserk distance:wu#The max range at which we will go berserk if we see a friendly AI of the same type (brute, etc) get killed
peer killed berserk chance:[0,1]#Chance that we will go berserk if we see a friendly AI of the same type (brute, etc) with the same or lower standing get killed
leader killed berserk chance:[0,1]#Chance that we will go berserk if we see a friendly AI of the same type (brute, etc) with higher standing get killed
berserk weapon#when I berserk, I pull out a ...
Beserk cooldown:seconds#Time that I will stay in beserk after losing my target, and then revert back to normal

proximity berserk range:world units#If our target is closer than this distance, and not (in a vehicle/larger size than us/using a melee weapon), we will berserk. If our target is further than this distance, we will stop berserking. Set to 0 to disable proximity berserking.
proximity berserk outside fp range:world units#We will never go more than this far outside our firing point areas when proximity-berserking. 0= no limit.
proximity berserk chance:[0,1]#If we have a target close enough to berserk, this is the chance that we will do so. If chance fails, we will try again after timeout.
proximity berserk timeout:seconds#We will not proximity-berserk unless it has been at least this long since we last stopped berserking. 0= no timeout.
Broken kamikaze chance#Probability that I will run the kamikaze behaviour when my leader dies.
Perimeter Range#How far we will melee charge outside our firing points before starting perimeter (defaults to 5wu)
Perimeter Range Close#How far we will melee charge outside our firing points before starting perimeter when the target is close to me (within 3wu) (defaults to 9wu)
Perimeter Damage Timeout:secs#How long will we take damage from our target before either seeking cover or berserking (defaults to 3secs)
difficulty limits
Difficulty Limits
fields.definition: Per difficulty values to limit the number of guys in particular behaviours

fields.name: max kamikaze count#How many guys in a single clump can be kamikazing at one time
max berserk count#How many guys in a single clump can be berserking at one time
min berserk count#We'd like at least this number of guys in a single clump can be berserking at one time (primarily combat forms)
Evasion danger threshold#Consider evading when immediate danger surpasses threshold
Evasion delay timer#Wait at least this delay between evasions
Evasion chance#If danger is above threshold, the chance that we will evade. Expressed as chance of evading within a 1 second time period
Evasion proximity threshold#If target is within given proximity, possibly evade
dive retreat chance#Chance of retreating (fleeing) after danger avoidance dive
cover flags
hide behind cover time:seconds#how long we stay behind cover after seeking cover
Hologram conditions
fields.definition: The following fields dictate how hologram equipment is used by AI in cover.

fields.name: hologram cover wait time:seconds#how long we wait in cover before using the hologram
hologram cooldown delay:seconds#Amount of time I will wait before trying to use hologram equipment again (0 value defaults to 5 seconds)
Cover conditions
fields.definition: The following fields dictate the conditions under which we will go into cover.

fields.name: Cover shield fraction#Only cover when shield falls below this level
Cover vitality threshold#Only cover when vitality falls below this level
Cover danger threshold#Danger must be this high to cover.
Aggresive/Defensive cover properties
minimum defensive distance from target:wus#How far from the target should we switch from aggresive to defensive covering (0 always defensive, big number always offensive)
minimum defensive distance from cover:wus#If our cover point is less than this distance, we will never consider defensive covering
always defensive scary threshold#If the target has scarines bigger or equal to this, we will always cover defensively
Other
Cover check delay:seconds#Amount of time I will wait before trying again after covering (0 value defaults to 2 seconds)
Cover pinned down check delay:seconds#Amount of time I will wait before issuing a pinned down message (0 value defaults to 2 seconds)
Emerge from cover when shield fraction reaches threshold#Emerge from cover when shield fraction reaches threshold
Proximity self-preserve:wus#Triggers self-preservation when target within this distance (assuming proximity_self_preserve_impulse is enabled)
proximity melee distance#When self preserving from a target less than given distance, causes melee attack (assuming proximity_melee_impulse is enabled)
unreachable enemy danger threshold#When danger from an unreachable enemy surpasses threshold, actor cover (assuming unreachable_enemy_cover impulse is enabled)
unassailable enemy danger threshold#When target is unassailable, and danger goes over this value - cover (assuming unassailable_enemy_cover impulse is enabled)
scary target threshold#When target is aware of me and surpasses the given scariness, self-preserve (assuming scary_target_cover_impulse is enabled)

Vitality fraction shield equipment{Vitality fraction bubbleshield}#Fraction of vitality below which an equipped shield equipment (instant cover/bubbleshield) will be activated (once damage has died down, and assuming shield_equipment_impulse is enabled)
Recent damage shield equipment#Must have less than this amount of recent body damage before we can deploy our equipped shield equipment.
Retreat flags
Shield threshold#When shield vitality drops below given amount, retreat is triggered by low_shield_retreat_impulse
Scary target threshold#When confronting an enemy of over the given scariness, retreat is triggered by scary_target_retreat_impulse
Danger threshold#When perceived danger rises above the given threshold, retreat is triggered by danger_retreat_impulse
Proximity threshold#When enemy closer than given threshold, retreat is triggered by proximity_retreat_impulse
min/max forced cower time bounds#actor cowers for at least the given amount of time
min/max cower timeout bounds#actor times out of cower after the given amount of time
proximity ambush threshold#If target reaches is within the given proximity, an ambush is triggered by the proximity ambush impulse
awareness ambush threshold#If target is less than threshold (0-1) aware of me, an ambush is triggered by the vulnerable enemy ambush impulse
leader dead retreat chance#If leader-dead-retreat-impulse is active, gives the chance that we will flee when our leader dies within 4 world units of us
peer dead retreat chance#If peer-dead-retreat-impulse is active, gives the chance that we will flee when one of our peers (friend of the same race) dies within 4 world units of us
second peer dead retreat chance#If peer-dead-retreat-impulse is active, gives the chance that we will flee when a second peer (friend of the same race) dies within 4 world units of us
flee timeout:seconds#Flee for no longer than this time (if there is no cover, then we will keep fleeing indefinitely). Value of 0 means 'no timeout'
zig-zag angle:degrees#The angle from the intended destination direction that a zig-zag will cause
zig-zag period:seconds#How long it takes to zig left and then zag right.
retreat grenade chance#The likelihood of throwing down a grenade to cover our retreat
backup weapon#If I want to flee and I don't have flee animations with my current weapon, throw it away and try a ...
Search flags
search time

Search distance#Maximum distance away from our firing positions that we are happy to search (0 value will default to 3wu). Does not affect vehicle search distance (see maxd if you want that value too).
Uncover
Uncover distance bounds#Distance of uncover point from target. Hard lower limit, soft upper limit.
Investigate
fields.definition: Orphan offset: Offset from the orphan pos along the orphan hint vector that we will investigate to. Positive value means we are predicting where the target is going, negative value means that we would like to stand back a bit and look along the orphan vector.

fields.name: Orphan offset:wu#(0 value will default to 1.8wu)
Minimum offset:wu#Minimum offset from the target point to investigate, otherwise we just use the target point itself. Not entirely sure about the justification for this one...
vocalization time

Performance Cool-down Time#The number of seconds that must elapse before an actor will consider a search-performance again

max presearch time:seconds#Presearch turns off after the given time
max suppress time:seconds#Suppress turns off after the given time (0 defaults to 8 seconds)

ZTG
idle pose delay time:seconds#time range for delays between idle poses
wander delay time:seconds#time to pause at a point while wandering
Skip Fraction
fields.definition:
For vocalization with priority < react, multiplies the skip fraction in the dialogue globals tag.
For priorities > react, multiplies the vocalization WEIGHT (i.e. the desire of that character to vocalize RELATIVE to other characters in the scene).
Thus if you hurt the arbiter, say, he's still going to yell at you, no matter what, and if you kill somebody and nobody else is there to witness it, he will yell at you.
As always, 0 means speak normally, 1 means skip everything (don't say shit)

fields.name: character skip fraction:[0,1]
look comment time:s#How long does the player look at an AI before the AI responds?
look long comment time:s#How long does the player look at the AI before he responds with his 'long look' comment?

Combat ranges
maximum firing range:world units#we can only fire our weapon at targets within this distance
minimum firing range#weapon will not be fired at target closer than given distance
normal combat range:world units
bombardment range#we offset our burst targets randomly by this range when firing at non-visible enemies (zero = never)
Max special target distance:world units#Specific target regions on a vehicle or unit will be fired upon only under the given distance
Ballistic Firing
super-ballistic range#we try to aim our shots super-ballistically if target is outside this range (zero = never)
Ballistic firing bounds:world units#At the min range, the min ballistic fraction is used, at the max, the max ballistic fraction is used

special projectile error:degrees#projectile error angle for special weapon fire (applied in addition to the normal error)
Weapon drop when killed
drop weapon loaded#amount of ammo loaded into the weapon that we drop (in fractions of a clip, e.g. 0.3 to 0.5)
drop weapon ammo#total number of rounds in the weapon that we drop (ignored for energy weapons)
Accuracy
fields.definition:
Parameters control how accuracy changes over the duration of a series of bursts
Accuracy is an analog value between 0 and 1. At zero, the parameters of the first
firing-pattern block is used. At 1, the parameters in the second block is used. In
between, all the values are linearly interpolated

fields.name: normal accuracy bounds#Indicates starting and ending accuracies at normal difficulty
normal accuracy time#The amount of time it takes the accuracy to go from starting to ending
heroic accuracy bounds#Indicates starting and ending accuracies at heroic difficulty
heroic accuracy time#The amount of time it takes the accuracy to go from starting to ending
legendary accuracy bounds#Indicates starting and ending accuracies at legendary difficulty
legendary accuracy time#The amount of time it takes the accuracy to go from starting to ending
weapon melee damage
weapon^
firing patterns
rate of fire#how many times per second we pull the trigger (zero = continuously held down)
target tracking:[0,1]#how well our bursts track moving targets. 0.0= fire at the position they were standing when we started the burst. 1.0= fire at current position
target leading:[0,1]#how much we lead moving targets. 0.0= no prediction. 1.0= predict completely.
burst geometry
fields.definition:
at the start of every burst we pick a random point near the target to fire at, on either the left or the right side.
the burst origin angle controls whether this error is exactly horizontal or might have some vertical component.

over the course of the burst we move our projectiles back in the opposite direction towards the target. this return motion is also controlled by an angle that specifies how close to the horizontal it is.

for example if the burst origin angle and the burst return angle were both zero, and the return length was the same as the burst length, every burst would start the same amount away from the target (on either the left or right) and move back to exactly over the target at the end of the burst.

fields.name: burst origin radius:world units#how far away from the target the starting point is
burst origin angle:degrees#the range from the horizontal that our starting error can be
burst return length:world units#how far the burst point moves back towards the target (could be negative)
burst return angle:degrees#the range from the horizontal that the return direction can be
burst duration:seconds#how long each burst we fire is
burst separation:seconds#how long we wait between bursts
weapon damage modifier#what fraction of its normal damage our weapon inflicts (zero = no modifier)
projectile error:degrees#error added to every projectile we fire
burst angular velocity:degrees per second#the maximum rate at which we can sweep our fire (zero = unlimited)
maximum error angle:degrees#cap on the maximum angle by which we will miss target (restriction on burst origin radius
grenades flags
grenade type^#type of grenades that we throw^
trajectory type#how we throw our grenades
YZNPI
minimum enemy count#how many enemies must be within the radius of the grenade before we will consider throwing there
enemy radius:world units#we consider enemies within this radius when determining where to throw
grenade ideal velocity:world units per second#how fast we LIKE to throw our grenades
grenade velocity:world units per second#the fastest we can possibly throw our grenades
grenade ranges:world units#ranges within which we will consider throwing a grenade
collateral damage radius:world units#we won't throw if there are friendlies around our target within this range
grenade chance:[0,1]#how likely we are to throw a grenade in one second
grenade throw delay:seconds#How long we have to wait after throwing a grenade before we can throw another one
grenade uncover chance:[0,1]#how likely we are to throw a grenade to flush out a target in one second
anti-vehicle grenade chance:[0,1]#how likely we are to throw a grenade against a vehicle

grenade count#number of grenades that we start with
dont drop grenades chance:[0,1]#how likely we are not to drop any grenades when we die, even if we still have some

Flying Avoidance
lookahead_time:seconds# How much in the future should we estimate a collision (affects collision ray length)
roll change magnitude:degrees#How fast to control the roll avoidance
roll decay multiplier:(0,1)#How fast roll goes back to 0- 1 means never, 0 means instantaneously
throttle grace period:seconds#How long after a collision should the vehicle keep moving at max throttle
minimum throttle:(-1,1)#Minimum throttle that the avoidance can slow down to

Hover Perturbation New
hover deceleration distance: wus#Distance defines a box outside of which we clamp perturbation. Value of zero causes fallback to run.
hover offset distance: wus#The max radius of the XZ anchor perturbation. Value of zero causes fallback to run.
Hover Perturbation Fallback
hover allow perturbation speed: wu/s#The speed the vehicle must be below for it to start running hover perturbation (default=0.4).
hover random x-axis period: sec#The number of seconds for the x component of the anchor perturbation to repeat itself (default=10).
hover random y-axis period: sec#The number of seconds for the y component of the anchor perturbation to repeat itself (default=7).
hover random z-axis period: sec#The number of seconds for the z component of the anchor perturbation to repeat itself (default=5).
hover random radius: wu#The radius of the anchor perturbation. (default=0)
hover anchor approach speed limit: wu/s#If we're traveling faster than this amount toward the anchor inside the max throttle distance, we drop throttle to the min. (default=0.2)
hover anchor throttle scale distance: wu#The distance from the anchor at which the min and max throttle scale occur. (default=[0.25, 2.0])
hover anchor xy-throttle scale: [0,1]#The xy-throttle scale at the min and max throttle scale distances. (default=[0.0, 0.2])
hover anchor z-throttle scale: [0,1]#The z-throttle scale at the min and max throttle scale distances. (default=[1.0, 1.0])
hover throttle min z: [-1, 1]#The minimum the z component of throttle is allowed to be (default=0.2)

ai banshee steering maximum:(banshees)#

ai oversteering scale:(warthogs, ghosts, wraiths)

ai sideslip distance:(ghosts, dropships)#Distance within which Ai will strafe to target (as opposed to turning)
ai avoidance distance:world units:(banshees)#Look-ahead distance for obstacle avoidance
ai min urgency:[0-1] (warthogs, banshees)#The minimum urgency with which a turn can be made (urgency = percent of maximum steering delta)
destination behind angle:(dropships)#The angle from facing that is considered to be behind us (we do the ugly floaty slidey turn to things behind us)
skid scale:(warthogs)#When approaching a corner at speed, we may want to skid around that corner, by turning slightly too early. This is (roughly) how many seconds ahead we should start turning.
Throttle
ai throttle maximum:(0 - 1) (all vehicles)
ai reverse throttle maximum:(0 - 1) (ground only)#If zero, default to ai throttle maximum
ai goal min throttle scale: (warthogs, dropships, ghosts)#scale on throttle when within 'ai deceleration distance' of goal (0...1)
ai turn min throttle scale: (warthogs, dropships, ghosts)# Scale on throttle due to nearness to a turn (0...1)
ai direction min throttle scale: (warthogs, dropships, ghosts)# Scale on throttle due to facing away from intended direction (0...1)
ai skid min throttle scale: (warthogs, dropships, ghosts)# Scale on throttle due to skidding (0...1)
skid attentuation max angle#Maximise min throttle at this deviation of angles from current movement
ai acceleration scale:(0-1): (warthogs, ghosts)# The maximum allowable change in throttle between ticks
ai throttle blend:(0-1): (dropships, sentinels)# The degree of throttle blending between one tick and the next (0 = no blending)
theoretical max speed:wu/s (warthogs, dropships, ghosts)# About how fast I can go.
error scale: (warthogs, dropships)# scale on the difference between desired and actual speed, applied to throttle

ai charge tight angle distance: (all vehicles)# The distance at which the tight angle criterion is used for deciding to vehicle charge
ai charge tight angle:[0-1] (all vehicles)# Angle cosine within which the target must be when target is closer than tight angle distance in order to charge
ai charge repeat timeout: (all vehicles)# Time delay between vehicle charges
ai charge look-ahead time: (all vehicles)# In deciding when to abort vehicle charge, look ahead these many seconds to predict time of contact
ai charge consider distance: (all vehicles)# Consider charging the target when it is within this range (0 = infinite distance)
ai charge abort distance: (all vehicles)# Abort the charge when the target get more than this far away (0 = never abort)
ai charge armor locked target chance: [0-1] (all vehicles)#Probability that we decide to charge a target even if they are armor locked
vehicle ram timeout#The ram behavior stops after a maximum of the given number of seconds

ai cover damage threshold: (all vehicles)# Trigger a cover when recent damage is above given threshold (damage_vehicle_cover impulse)
ai cover shield damage threshold: (all vehicles)# Trigger a cover when recent shied damage is above given threshold (flying_cover behavior)

ai cover min boost distance: (all vehicles)# Boosting allowed when distance to cover destination is greater then this.
turtling recent damage threshold:%#If vehicle turtling behavior is enabled, turtling is initiated if 'recent damage' surpasses the given threshold

Default Values
Vector Weights
fields.definition: Think of this as firing point evaluation; how much do we want to weight each of the various vectors affecting our movement in space.

fields.name: facing#How much our current movement vector affects our new vector selection.
perturbation#How much random deviation is applied to our vector selection.
volume avoidance#How strongly we avoid our containing volume.
volume perturbation#How strongly we are randomly perturbed by our containing volume.
volume cover#How strongly are we attracted toward cover points.
flocking#How much of a tendancy to flock we have.
target#The weight of our movement towards our current target.
target tail#The weight of our movement intended to keep our target in our tail.
Area Selection
area reselect time:seconds#The time bounds on how long we should stay in an area before moving to another area.
Idle
idle time:seconds#How long before reselecting a destination while idling.
Cover
unsafe cover reselect time:seconds#How long after being exposed in cover before we reselect new cover.
cover heading reselect time:seconds#The frequency at which we pick a new cover point on our current piece of cover.
max cover search distance:wu#The farthest from ourselves that we will search for cover.
max cover impulse distance:wu#The farthest our target can be from us when decided if we should go to cover.
spline cooldown time:wu#The number of seconds we wait after traveling a spline to travel a spline again.
Volume Avoidance
volume influence distance:wu#How far our volume influences our movement
volume perturbation phase:seconds#What is the frequency of oscillation of our volume perturbation vector
volume bounding distance:wu#How far we are allowed outside our volume before we started being forced to return (must be >= 0)
Approach
volume approach distance:wu#If our target is this close to our containing volume, then start attacking him
volume break off distance:wu#While attacking our target, if we get this far outside our containing volume, break off the attack and return home
minimum approach distance:wu#When this close to our target we will drop into the evade behaviour instead
collision avoidance range:wu#Zero collision avoidance at the high distance, Max avoidance at the low distance.
Evasion
evade time:seconds#Bounds on how long we should evade for
evade body damage threshold:[0,1]#The recent body damage we should sustain before trying to evade.
evade shield damage threshold:[0,1]#The recent shield damage we should sustain before trying to evade.
bogey retreat time:seconds#How long we tolerate a bogey in our six, before we retreat back to our area.
bogey retreat distance:wu#How close a bogey has to be before we'll even consider retreating.
Flocking
flock radius:wu#Distance controls for flocking
forward follow angle:degrees#How close we must be facing another friend to consider following him during flocking.
behind follow angle:degrees#The angle of the 'cone' behind a friend who I am interested in following that I must be in to consider him during flocking.
Tailing
min tailing time:seconds#The minimum amount of time we can be tailing
tailing radius:wu#Distance controls for tailing
tailing cone angle:degrees#The angle of the 'cone' behind a foe who I am interested in tailing after approaching.
Strafing
volume strafe distance:wu#If our target is this close to our containing volume, then start strafing him
strafe min distance:wu#I need to be at least this far away from my target to consider strafing him (2D)
strafe above distance:wu#How high above our target we will aim for when strafing
strafe abort distance:wu#If I get this close to my target, stop strafing and retreat for a bit (2D)
strafe timeout:seconds#How long we go between strafes
Attitude Control
max descend angle:degrees#The maximum angle at which we can descend.
max ascend angle:degrees#The maximum angle at which we can ascend.
Shooting
shooting cone angle:degrees#The angle of the shooting cone along the vehicle facing.
Evasive Maneuvers
missile dodge change:percentage#The chance that an AI will dodge incoming missiles.
ideal missile dodge distance:wu#The ideal distance a trick should take you away from danger.

distance damage outer radius#Considered damaging-outside-range when you START firing from outside this distance
distance damage inner radius#Considered damaging-outside-range when you CONTINUE firing from outside this distance
distance damage time#Damaging tank guy from outside-range for this long causes a morph
distance damage reset time#Damage timer is reset after this long of not damaging him from outside-range
throttle distance#Throttle the tank from running (far) to walking (near) across this range of distances. (defaults to 5 and 3)
protect damage amount#Once current damage reaches this amount, protect your special parts until no recent damage
protect time:seconds#How long should we protect our special parts for?
spew infection character#What character should I throw up all over my target? Carrots?

Stealth dude
stealth morph distance threshold#Morphing inside this range causes a tank guy, outside this range causes a ranged fella
stealth morph damage threshold#Percentage of body health he has to be taken down in order to cause a morph
stalk range min#We want to stalk our target from outside this radius
stalk range max#We want to stalk our target from inside this radius
stalk range hard max#We will never be able to pick a firing position more than this far from our target
stalk charge chance#While stalking, charge randomly with this probability per second (also will charge when on periphery, this is just some spice)
Ranged bloke
ranged proximity distance#Morph to tank/stalker when someone gets this close to me as a ranged form
turtle damage threshold#amount of damage necessary to trigger a turtle
turtle time#when turtling, turtle for a random time with these bounds:seconds

turtle abort distance#when my target get within this range, abort turtling
General
group morph range#Follow the morph of any other form within this distance
Equipment
fields.definition: Create one of these for each equipment type that you would like a character to be able to use. If you specify a relative drop chance of greater than zero then the character will drop this type of equipment with that (relative) chance. If you leave the equipment field empty, then there will be that (relative) chance of dropping no equipment at all.

fields.name: equipment^#The equipment item that is to be usable
flags
relative drop chance#The relative chance of this equipment being dropped with respect to the other pieces of equipment specified in this block
equipment use
Equipment Use
fields.definition: How should I use this equipment?

fields.name: use when^#When should we use this equipment?
use how#How should we use this equipment?
Skip Fraction
fields.definition: Given that we have decided to use this equipment, should we do so? (0= always use it, 1= never use it). Heroic skip fraction is the average of normal and legendary chances.

fields.name: easy/normal:0-1#
legendary:0-1#

fields.definition: Here you can define a character tag which will be applied on top of this one when the named stimulus is present on a character.In the case of multiple stimulus with character tag responses being present, the first listed will be used.

Pain Reactions

pain screen duration:seconds#The duration of the pain function
0 defaults to 0.5
pain screen region fade out duration:seconds|CCBBAA#The time it takes to fade out a damage region that is no longer the most recent damage region to be hit
pain screen region fade out weight threshold:[0,1]#The threshold weight below which the focus channel must fall before we can cross fade to another region.
pain screen angle tolerance:degrees#The tolerance angle between next and previous damage directions, below which we randomly vary the ping direction.
pain screen angle randomness:degrees#The maximum random angle to vary the incoming ping direction by if it's too close to the previous ping.
Defensive Reactions

defensive screen duration:seconds#The duration of the defensive function
0 defaults to 2.0
defensive screen scrub fallback fraction:[0,1]#When receiving multiple pings, this is the min percentage of the defensive screen scrub value will fallback to.

options: none - actor has no targets whatsoever
dead enemy- my only target is a dead enemy
non-attacking enemy- I am debilitated and the enemy is not attacking me
disregarded orphan- did not find my target and gave up searching
inspected orphan- checked last know position and didn't find target; still searching
uninspected orphan- can't see target, but have a good idea where they may be
certain orphan- can't see target, but know exactly where they are
visible enemy- I can see the target
nearby enemy- I can see the target, and it's close
potentially dangerous enemy- target is near and is facing me
dangerous enemy- target is facing my way and fighting
attacking enemy- target is aiming at me and shooting me
very close enemy- enemy is really close
lower weapon when no alert movement override
Unassailable cover ends ONLY when target assailable
force aim from barrel position#use only for weapons with really, really long barrels (bfg), do NOT use for rotating turret weapons (warthog, falcon, etc)
